REFERRI NG PHYSI a AN: M Z. Jasser MD
ADM TTI NG PHYSI a AN: DO John Yu DO
CHI EF COMPLAI NT: Fall, right hip pain.
HI STORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: A 79-year-old male. He was seen at the rehab. Patient had
recent left hammertoe surgery on the left foot with podiatry. Patient was told to be
non-weight bearing primarily on the left foot. However, he was using a knee scooter and
was using the toilet. He sat down and then when trying to get up and not put weight on
his left foot, the patient fell onto his right hip. Patient suffered a right proximal
f emur fracture. Patient was admitted to Honor Health Deer Valley. He underwent right hip
intramedullary nailing by Dr. Wong on 03/08. Patient's creatinine was a little bit
elevated. He was found to have some urinary retention. Patient does have history of
prior prostate procedure. Workup showed that he had an intraluminal | adder mass, likely
arising fromthe prostate and on CT on 03/24. He was seen by urology. They had
difficulty passing a catheter. Patient had some retention but then was able to urinate.
Creatinine improved to 1.1. Patient was recommended outpatient cysto and follow-up.
Patient had some postoperative anemia, did require a unit of bl ood postoperatively.
Patient also had some electrolyte inbal ances, hypokal hypomagnesem a that was
repleted. He had a couple episodes of hyper gl ycemi a, A1c of 5.4 and his bl ood sugar did
stabilize. He was recommended outpatient follow with bone and health clinic for his
PAST MEDI CAL HI STORY: BPH, history of prior procedure on his prostate, history of
ALLERGI ES: No known drug allergies.
Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Scottsdale
MEDI CATI ONS: On admission ude: Tylenol, aspirin 81 mg b.i.d. for DVT prophylaxis,
um carbonate, cholecalciferol, docusate, idocaine pat ch, methocarbamol, oxycodone,
soa AL HI STORY: The patient is a nonsmoker, denies t obacco, heavy al cohol, or illicit
drug use. Patient is a Vietnamveteran, lives with his family.
FAMILY HI STORY: Noncontributory to the current presentation.
REVI EW OF SYSTEMS: A 12-point systems reviewed and negative except as detailed in HPI.
VI TAL SI GNS: Blood pressure 130/74, pulse 83, respirations 18, temperature 98.4.
GENERAL: This is a well-nourished, well-developed male, in no acute distress.
HEENT: Pupils equal, round, reactive tolight and accompdation. Extraocular movements
intact. Micous membranes are pink and moist.
NECK: Supple, no JVD or thyromegaly.
HEART: Normal S1, S2, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
ABDOMEN: Soft, nontender, with no visceromegaly.
EXTREM TI ES: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. Patient has a dressing, surgical dressing
over the right anterior hip. Patient is also wearing a camtype boot on the left foot and
1. Right f emur fracture, status post ORI F. Patient will continue on aspirin b.i.d. for
DVT prophylaxis. Patient is on oxycodone, Tylenol for pain control, also on metaxal one as
needed. PMT\R has been consulted. PT/OT will continue to work with patient.
2. Postoperative anemia. we will follow hemoglobin.
3. Patient with enlarged prostate, had some urinary retention. Foley not able to be
replaced. Creatinine was stable, 1-1.1 range. He has some gement of the prostate,
possible extending into the bladder on a CT. Patient will need outpatient urology
4. Patient found to have an incidental small left pleural effusion. No need outpatient
monitoring of this. No signs of respiratory compromise. Patient will remain on aspirin
5. Patient had a recent left foot hammertoe surgery. Patient with podiatry. Patient is
to be non-weight bearing on the left foot to allowfor healing.
6. Patient had some BPH and urinary retention. He on Flomax. We will monitor urine

This patient is currently under my care, and I have initiated the establishment of a plan of care
for home health services following the face-to-face encounter on the date noted above. I certify
that this patient is confined to his home and needs intermittent skilled services (nursing care,
physical therapy and/or speech therapy or continues to need occupational therapy) as indicated in
the above summary of impaired body structures that supports the homebound status and need for
skilled care. This patient will be followed by his primary care provider who will be responsible for
further review of the plan of care and recertification as applicable.
